title: Chosenia bracteosa
taxonomy: Chosenia bracteosa is classified within the family Oleaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the olive or lilac family. This family consists of around 600 species, including various types of trees and shrubs. The genus Chosenia, to which Chosenia bracteosa belongs, is a small group of plants primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere. Chosenia bracteosa is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Its classification is further refined to the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants.
uses: Chosenia bracteosa is a plant used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia. Its bark and leaves are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In some cultures, Chosenia bracteosa is also used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its efficacy in treating various health conditions.

The plant's berries, leaves, and bark are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits.
